source.definition: feeling deep satisfaction and pleasure in one's own or others' achievements or qualities

explanation.sourceText: The English word "proud" comes from Old English "prūt", meaning 'brave' or 'valiant', and evolved to express a sense of self-respect and satisfaction.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „proud” provine din engleza veche „prūt”, care însemna „curajos” sau „viteaz” și a evoluat pentru a exprima un sentiment de respect de sine și satisfacție.

source.additional: Commonly used to express positive feelings about personal or others' accomplishments.

target.definition: o senzație pozitivă de satisfacție față de realizările proprii sau ale altora

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "mândru" derives from the Slavic "mindre", meaning "proud", closely connected to the feeling of satisfaction.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „mândru“ provine din slavonul „mindre“, care înseamnă „mândru“ și este legat de sentimentul de satisfacție.

target.additional: Oft genutzt, um persönliche Erfolge oder Errungenschaften zu beschreiben.

explanation.sourceText: Used when acknowledging the success of others.
explanation.targetText: Folosit când cineva recunoaște succesul altora.

examples.sourceText: I am proud of my friends.

explanation.sourceText: Used when showing respect towards someone.
explanation.targetText: Folosit când cineva are respect față de altcineva.

examples.sourceText: Many people are proud of their teacher.
examples.targetText: Mulți oameni sunt mândri de profesorul lor.

examples.sourceText: I am proud of my achievement.
She was proud of her children.
examples.targetText: Ea era mândră de copiii ei.

examples.sourceText: He is proud of his country.
examples.targetText: El este mândru de țara sa.

examples.sourceText: We are proud of our traditions.
She was proud of her school achievements.
examples.targetText: Ea era mândră de succesele ei la școală.

examples.sourceText: He said that he was proud.
examples.targetText: El a spus că este mândru.

learningTips.text: Remember "mândru" by thinking of 'mandrill', a proud and colorful monkey – imagine being proud like a mandrill showing off its colors!
Link "mândru" to 'man drew' – imagine a man who drew a masterpiece and feels proud of his work.
Think of 'mândru' sounding like 'mandate' – you feel proud when you have a mandate or responsibility.
Associate "mândru" with 'Monday' – start your week proud of your achievements from the weekend.
